Request for substantive amendments to an existing development agreement for 6324 & 6330 Quinpool Road, Halifax to allow an additional two storeys to the approved 8 storey building.

This variance request will avoid the costly relocation or demolition of the existing main building while maintaining an additional unit of housing supply that meets the size and lot coverage requirements of what could be built in its place as a backyard suite, while allowing me to develop the rest of the lot to add a 2-unit stand-alone dwelling in adherance with today's codes and setbacks/LUBs.
Current_Land_Use: My property consists of a 403 square-meter lot with a small two-story 80 square-meter house built in the 1950s. The house has a footprint of 50 square meters and covers 12% of the lot. It is situated between 0.9m and 1.5m from the east lot line, over 1.9 meters from the west lot line, and between 0.25m and 2.3m from the rear south lot line.  The property is currently zoned for R-2 but only has one
Proposed_Land_Use:
I am proposing maintaining the existing land-use of R-2 but maximizing the number of units on the lot to a 2-unit main building plus a back-yard suite.  In order to achieve this, I am proposing reclassifying the current main building to an accessory structure/backyard suite and requesting a variance to maintain the current setbacks to avoid relocating the main building by a couple of meters or having to tear it down. 



This will allow for a new stand-alone two-unit main building to be constructed, adding much-needed density to the Halifax area in the form of 'Gentle Density' and 'Missing Middle Housing'. The new two-unit main building will be placed towards the front of the lot, adhering to all current codes, setbacks, and land-use bylaws.

the property was acquired in 1959 by the current owner's late grandfather and the dwelling was built in 1960 as a cottage only. There are four other properties that are situated on a private right of way with deeded access to each. Because of the location of an extremely large rock, the right of way was moved to one side (closer to the landowners property). In all of the years that the property owners have been in that location, the right of way has never been moved to it's proper location but has continued to be used regardless. When drawing up the plans for the addition and garage it was based on where the right of way should have been, not where it is.Right now it is showing a 0.38 m setback and the owners require 8 feet. The foundation and frostwall was already put in as they were going by the original right of way markers, not what has been getting used. It has never been an issue because they are the second last property on the road and they are not impeding anyone's access. Both properties including their neighbour have been in use for almost 65 years. Hence the landowners are looking to have that variance changed from the 8 feet to the .38 m setback accommodate the current construction. All of the plans were done based on those existing measurements, unaware that they required that 8 foot measurement. thank you
Location: 31 JUNIPER LANE, UPPER LAKEVILLE, NS B0J1W0

We are a Health & Wellness Lifestyle Brand designed for an elite future.



Our goal is to create a place/environment for our community that fosters health and wellness and supports those who work hard and strive to reach their passions and goals. 



For a healthy lifestyle our community needs better resources and we are passionate about making this happen by means of our elements.



FUN



We plan to create a space where like-minded individuals who are passionate about their lives and goals can come together and strive together. Throughout the year we will offer a variety of fun wellness workshops and events where our members will be able to connect and become part of a larger community.



FITNESS



Paired with proper fuelling, moving the body in different ways is important for overall well-being. Our goal is to provide our clients with as many avenues to move their body as possible! This way there is something for everyone; from people just starting their wellness journey to high level athletes looking for specialized training. In the future our space will offer a variety of classes including Yoga, Mobility, Pilates, Barre, HIIT, Group Fusion Fitness, Bootcamp, as well as Recovery Classes such as foam rolling and myofascial release sessions.



FUEL



Meal Prep | Cold Pressed Juice | Protein Snacks 



We are elite beings and we deserve high-quality fuel to support our overall well-being.



Our food is Chef Created, Athlete Inspired & Dietician Designed. Made with simple ingredients such as local produce and free-range/grass-fed protein sources our products will give you all the macros you need to properly function including healthy fats, proteins and carbohydrates.

Proposed_Activity_Description: Application by Matthew and Jordyn Howse to enter into a development agreement to allow a café use at 204 Conrad Road, Lawrencetown.
Current_Land_Use: The land is currently zoned RR-1 mixed-use commercial with a specification for I-2 Carpentry. We have been operating at take-out only cafe for the past two years on a misunderstanding that we could run this home-based business without an amendment.
Proposed_Land_Use:
The proposed use is to renovated the commercial building and bring it up to modern building code with an A-2 classification to operate an a cafe with a seating area for customers inside. We we would be seeking to remain an RR-1 zoning with a variance for business use of cafe and bakery. We are seeking the classifications of restaurant - full service to enable the seating area but would not be a full scale restaurant that offers dinner service, alcohol, etc.



The proposed activity of Better Vibe Cafe would be a kitchen that produces ice cream, coffee and other non-alcoholic beverages, baked goods, and other food. Customers have the option to order take-out or a small dine in seating area to accommodate for weather. Many of our customers are cyclists from the trail or dog owners coming to and from the beach so an outdoor seating area is largely beneficial.



Since operating as a take-out we have become a staple in the neighborhood and a meeting place for most of the residents of Conrad road and its side roads. As such, many of our customers arrive on foot from the street, on bike from the trail and neighbouring roads, or on foot from their on street parking space on their walk to Conrad Beach,

Application by Charles Freeman Architect to enter into a development agreement that allows for a commercial recreation use (stargazing facility) at 5933 Prospect Road, Big Lake.
Current_Land_Use: The land is currently operating under 'open space use' under the bylaw. That is, it is free of buildings and is undeveloped except for a single driveway.
Proposed_Land_Use:
The proposed land use is primarily 'Commerical Recreation Use', with a secondary use of 'Institutional Use' for some buildings. The DA reinforces existing as-of-right uses for 'Single Unit Dwelling Use' and 'Craft Shop Use' that the current land use bylaw (LUB) allows. All of the aforementioned uses are as defined in the current LUB.



As expounded in the DA rationale, the proposed Stargazing Park is a facility that allows users, local and tourists alike, to engage in stargazing, and to learn about stargazing while enjoying the natural beauty of the site. A secondary use of 'institutional' allows professional astronomers and astronauts to use the facility as well as allowing space-related societies and groups to gather to share idea and engage in stargazing and associated analog research. Also under 'institutional use', the proposed DA allows for local community groups to use the multi-purpose rooms and the larger site for community events.

Proposed_Activity_Description:
Variance for Permit BPRES-2024-06766



In my original plan submitted the measurements on the flanking side of the lot were taken from where the grass and curb meet. It has been brought to my attention the measurements needed to be taken from the property pin. I have attached the site plan, revised measurements are in red.



This cannot be avoided as there are no other places on my lot to accommodate the structure. 

I had thought about moving to another location but with the current housing crisis it is more financially responsible to upgrade my current property than to try and purchase a new larger home/property.



If my house was not on a corner lot the proposed structure would meet all of HRM’s criteria. (There is already a driveway opening in the sidewalk on that side of the property.



The proposed structure would be 24’ from the grass/curb line and 15’ from the property pin.
